items.title: 【GUCCI】7月19日は最強開運日！優美なカラーの新作財布やカードケースに注目
items.summary: Gucci timed a Japan-exclusive small leather goods drop (GG Marmont zip/bifold wallets, card cases, key cases in light pink/light blue, plus a world-first Japan pre-release Gucci Lettering pouch and wallet) to July 19, 2026 - a rare triple 'lucky day' (Tensha-bi, Ichiryu-Manbaiki, Taian) on the Japanese calendar. Retail activation/PR coverage, not organic consumer commentary.

items.title: 「グッチ フローラ」が新フレグランス発売 香水着想の限定メイクアップコレクションも
items.summary: Gucci Beauty announced a new Gucci Flora Gorgeous Orchid Eau de Parfum Intense (launching September 1, 2026, JPY 5,170-24,970) plus a limited fragrance-inspired makeup collection (eyeshadow palette, two lipsticks), fronted by Miley Cyrus as campaign face. Brand-originated product/campaign news.

items.title: 루이비통도 다 뺀다…시내면세점 떠나는 명품
items.summary: Louis Vuitton is withdrawing from all three remaining Seoul downtown duty-free operators (Lotte, Shilla, Shinsegae), following Gucci, which the article notes already exited Shilla Seoul and Lotte World Tower duty-free outlets last year. Cited causes: shifting FIT tourist shopping toward pop-ups, won weakness making duty-free look pricier than department stores, and weak wholesale-buyer traffic - a structural retreat signal for luxury distribution in Korea, Gucci included.

items.title: 디자이너, CEO도 바꿔도 비틀대는 구찌, 1분기 매출 -8%
items.summary: Kering's Q1 2026 results showed Gucci revenue down 8% YoY to EUR 1.347bn even after replacing both creative director (Demna) and CEO (Francesca Bettoni); Japan fell steepest at -14%, Middle East -11% on regional conflict drag, while North America grew over 8%. Kering group sales fell 6% overall; management cited 'uncertain geopolitical and macroeconomic conditions.' Critical/financial-performance angle still being cited and recirculated in Korean trade coverage into Q3.

items.title: 구찌의 몰락, 수백억 달러 브랜드의 정체성 상실
items.summary: Feature-length retrospective on Gucci's 2025 collapse: revenue -22% to EUR 5.992bn, operating profit -40%, margin down to 16% (worst in 5 years), brand value -35% to $11.6bn (rank 69 globally). Traces the slide to loss of creative identity since Alessandro Michele's 2022 exit, two failed successor eras (De Sarno, then Demna's under-noticed Feb 2026 Milan debut), four CEO changes in two years, and China demand weakness; 25 stores closed in 2025 with 100+ more planned. Still being referenced/recirculated in Korean fashion-business commentary as of mid-2026.

items.title: 카톡으로 사는 샤넬, 네이버로 사는 구찌…콧대 낮춘 명품
items.summary: Report on luxury brands, including Gucci, lowering their traditional distribution posture by selling through KakaoTalk gifting and Naver commerce channels in Korea - read by the article as a sign of brands chasing volume/reach amid softer demand rather than pure exclusivity positioning; includes a note that Gucci raised prices on select handbags (Ophidia medium GG tote ~8%, Ophidia small shoulder ~6.5%, Jackie small shoulder ~5%) even as it pursues these lower-friction sales channels.

items.source: Sina Entertainment (via Marie Claire China licensing)
items.title: 张凌赫嘉人七月刊封面
items.summary: Chinese actor and Gucci brand ambassador Zhang Linghe (张凌赫) featured on the July issue cover of Marie Claire China, styled in Gucci FW26 pieces (black-and-white leather look, white feather look) tied to Demna's debut collection. Ambassador/celebrity-styling coverage amplified across Weibo fan and entertainment accounts - flagged as brand-adjacent activation buzz, not independent consumer sentiment.

items.title: 张凌赫出席Gucci时装秀
items.summary: Recirculating Weibo/entertainment coverage of Zhang Linghe attending a Gucci runway show wearing Demna's debut-era leather look; fan commentary (superlative praise of his styling) drives repost volume around the #古驰品牌大使张凌赫# hashtag. Included as a still-active SNS volume signal for the region despite original May date; treated as ambassador activation, magnitude-damped per sentiment rubric.

items.title: 不振のグッチ｢日本だけが好調｣その'カラクリ'
items.summary: Toyo Keizai analysis on why Gucci (and Louis Vuitton) keep raising prices repeatedly even as global sales soften, arguing Japan remains a relative bright spot for now due to yen-driven inbound tourist spending and lower relative price gaps versus other markets, but flags this as a fragile, currency-dependent explanation rather than genuine brand-health strength. Critical/analytical angle on the region's luxury pricing strategy.
